name: -(으)ㄴ/는 것 같아요 — It Seems / I Think

cards.grammarNote: Adjective + -(으)ㄴ 것 같아요 = it seems / looks like. 피곤하다 → 피곤한 것 같아요. 것 = thing/fact; 같아요 = seems like.
행복하다 (to be happy) → 행복한 것 같아요. Context (she) is inferred. Korean often omits the subject when clear.
화가 나다 (anger rises) → past modifier 난 (나 + ㄴ). 화가 난 것 같아요 = seems like anger has risen = seems angry.
Action verb + -는 것 같아요 (present). 무서워하다 (to feel scared) → 무서워하는 것 같아요. For adjectives use -(으)ㄴ; for action verbs use -는.
슬프다 (to be sad) → 슬픈 (슬프 + ㄴ → 슬픈). Adjective modifier form before 것 같아요.

cards.front: It looks like they're enjoying it.
cards.grammarNote: 즐기다 (to enjoy) → action verb, present tense modifier: 즐기는. 즐기는 것 같아요 = looks like (they're) enjoying it.
걱정하다 (to worry) → 걱정하는 것 같아요. Action verb → -는 것 같아요.
지루하다 (to be bored/boring) → 지루한 것 같아요. Adjective → -(으)ㄴ 것 같아요.

cards.front: It seems like he fell in love.
cards.grammarNote: 빠지다 (to fall into) → past modifier: 빠진 (빠지 + ㄴ). 사랑에 빠지다 = to fall in love. Past tense modifier used for completed states.
불편하다 (to be uncomfortable) → 불편한 것 같아요. 불편 = discomfort. Adjective form.
울다 (to cry) + 고 있다 (progressive) → 울고 있는 것 같아요. 있는 is the modifier form of 있다. Use progressive for ongoing actions.
나아지다 (to get better) → past modifier: 나아진 (나아지 + ㄴ). 기분 = mood/feeling. Combines past action modifier with 것 같아요.
